title: m-dev-tools repo manifest
description: Per-repo machine-readable manifest. Each m-dev-tools repo emits one of these at dist/repo.meta.json. The org-level catalog generator reads only this file and follows its `exposes` pointers — it never has to know what a 'command' or 'module' or 'rule' looks like internally.

id.description: Typed catalog ID. Phase 0 emits only tool-level IDs.

repo.description: Canonical GitHub URL of the repo.

role.description: One-sentence description of what the repo provides.

language.description: Primary languages used by the repo (e.g. ['python'], ['m'], ['typescript']).

agent_instructions.description: Path (relative to repo root) to the canonical agent-instructions file. Convention: 'AGENTS.md' with 'CLAUDE.md' symlinked to it.

verified_on.description: Date this manifest was last regenerated and verified accurate (YYYY-MM-DD).

exposes.description: Map from logical kind ('commands', 'modules', 'lint_rules', ...) to a path (relative to repo root, resolved by walking up from the manifest to find a .git ancestor) or absolute URL where the structured payload lives. For URL mode, the base is the repo's raw-content prefix (everything up to and including the branch). Repos declare what they own; the org generator follows these pointers.

consumes.description: Typed IDs of other repos this one depends on at build or runtime.

verification_commands.description: Shell commands an agent should run to verify a change in this repo (e.g. 'make check', 'm doctor').

status.description: Repo lifecycle status. Defaults to 'active' when omitted.

docs.description: Repo-level documentation metadata consumed by validate-docs.py (docs-discoverability spec §5.1). Optional — repos with only hand-written prose docs may omit this block entirely.

generated_paths.description: Glob patterns (relative to repo root, e.g. 'docs/modules/std*.md') for documentation files produced by a generator. Files matching these globs are exempt from the prose schema; validate-docs.py asserts only `generated: true` + `last_modified` drift against the source manifest. See docs-discoverability-spec.md §5.1.

notes.description: Free-text notes for human readers. Not consumed by the catalog generator.
